Prompt
U
Oracle Events
Search Slack #engineering (C003) for recent messages about the pipeline PR, config changes, and relaunch status using conversations_history (channel_id: C003) or conversations_search_messages (search_query: "config" or "pipeline" or "relaunch" or "ENG-217", filter_in_channel: C003). Discover three key messages: Anh Nguyen's config validation dry-run announcement (ts: 1776704400, April 20) stating everything looks clean for Vectral and Mosaic and pushing the build; Dmitri Volkov's message (ts: 1776788100, April 21) flagging a blocking review on Lena's ENG-217 pipeline hardening PR because of a race condition in validateSchema() where a shared lock on the schema cache is not held through the write phase, allowing a second config to slip through validation entirely; and Samira Tariq's relaunch readiness status check (ts: 1776882600, April 22) listing four blocking items for the April 30 go/no-go review with ENG-217, ENG-218, ENG-220, and ENG-212 all required to be green by EOD April 29.
Get thread replies on Anh Nguyen's config dry-run message using conversations_replies (channel_id: C003, thread_ts: "1776704400.000000"). This is the critical hidden discovery. Find Anh's follow-up reply (ts: 1777052820, April 24) where he admits his dry-run script has a side effect: it calls configStore.save() after loading each config into the preview renderer, and the save was writing to the live config store instead of a temp buffer. He checked that morning and the Mosaic config looks different from what he saw in Lena's fix PR, and the Vectral ergonomic limit is showing $400 instead of $600. This is the root cause of the config regression affecting both Vectral and Mosaic.
Search emails for messages related to the Vectral expense rejection using search_emails (query: "Vectral" or "expense" or "rejected" or "ergonomic" or similar). Find email_mina_forward_vectral_config_regression from mina.hashimoto@moveops.com to samira.tariq@moveops.com, CC lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com. Mina describes Danielle Osei's $490 ergonomic monitor arm being auto-rejected at a $400 limit, references Lena's March fix (ENG-214) that set it to $600, and asks engineering to investigate urgently and confirm whether the Mosaic config is also affected. Also discover the parent email email_rachel_vectral_config_regression_01 from rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com where Rachel escalates directly, stating this is the third time a Vectral employee has had a legitimate expense incorrectly rejected and that she is adding it to the list of service failures going to the board alongside the renewal decision.
Search emails for the unread vendor email about API errors using search_emails (query: "API" or "500" or "vendor" or "errors" or similar). Find email_greg_swift_api_errors from greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com to lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com, CC samira.tariq@moveops.com. Greg reports intermittent HTTP 500 errors on the vendor API since Tuesday April 22 affecting POST /api/v1/vendor/shipment-confirmation and POST /api/v1/vendor/delivery-receipt, with about 60% of POST requests failing. He has 4 active MoveOps shipments that week (GreenStack Mei-Lin Zhao hazmat, Mosaic Keiko Tanaka laser cutter, Vectral Camille Oduya, NorthWind Raj Patel server lab) and the manual workaround of faxing confirmations to Chloe is not sustainable. The errors started around 3 PM Pacific on April 22.
Retrieve the pipeline hardening ticket ENG-217 from Linear using linear_get_issue (id: "issue_pipeline_hardening"). Find the ticket assigned to moveops_lena_bjorkstrom, state in_progress, priority 1, under proj_expense_autocategorizer. The description lists four required changes: remove silent fallback to default templates, add strict type enforcement on JSON output, add a schema validation step before config deployment, and add PolicyValidator runtime type guards as a last-resort defense. Each has detailed acceptance criteria.
Read comments on ENG-217 using linear_list_comments (issueId: "issue_pipeline_hardening"). Review any existing comments for context on the ticket's progress. The full technical details of Dmitri's blocking review (race condition in validateSchema(), shared lock not held through write phase, second config could bypass validation during concurrent batch imports) come from his Slack message in C003 (ts: 1776788100). His Slack message mentions adding a detailed comment on the Linear ticket with reproduction steps, but that comment is not present in the data, so the agent must use the Slack message as the primary source for Dmitri's feedback.
Search Airtable stipend records for the rejected Vectral expense using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Stipend Transactions", field_name: "Account", value: "Vectral Systems" or field_name: "Employee Name", value: "Danielle Osei"). Find rec_stipend_danielle_monitor_arm with Employee Name: Danielle Osei, Account: Vectral Systems, Expense Category: Ergonomic, Amount Requested: $490, Approval Status: Rejected, Approved Amount: $0. The Review Notes reference a $400 ergonomic category limit from policy config vectral_systems_v1 with last_updated: 2026-04-20T17:01:12Z. The April 20 timestamp matches when Anh's script ran and overwrote the live config.
Check the calendar for the upcoming readiness review using calendar_search_events (query: "relaunch" or "readiness" or "ExpenseBot") or calendar_get_calendar_events_by_tag (tag: "engineering"). Find cal_expensebot_relaunch_review_apr30 scheduled for April 30 with attendees samira.tariq@moveops.com, lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com, dmitri.volkov@moveops.com, and anh.nguyen@moveops.com. The event description lists the four blocking items: ENG-217 pipeline hardening (Lena), ENG-218 dashboard type validation (Anh), ENG-220 duplicate receipt detection (Dmitri), and ENG-212 remaining edge case tests (Dmitri).
Look up Mina Hashimoto's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Mina Hashimoto"). Confirm mina.hashimoto@moveops.com as the correct address for the email reply.
Update Danielle Osei's Airtable stipend record using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblStipends00001, records: [{id: "rec_stipend_danielle_monitor_arm", fields with corrected approval status and a review note explaining the config regression caused the incorrect rejection}]). The note should explain that Anh's dry-run script on April 20 overwrote the live config store with normalized defaults, reverting the Vectral ergonomic limit from $600 to $400, which caused the false rejection of Danielle's $490 monitor arm.
Create a comment on ENG-217 (issue_pipeline_hardening) using linear_create_comment (issueId: "issue_pipeline_hardening"). The comment should address Dmitri's blocking review about the race condition in validateSchema(), acknowledge the shared lock issue during concurrent batch imports, and include a status update noting the discovery that Anh's dry-run script caused a config regression by writing normalized configs back to the live store instead of a temp buffer, which reinforces why the schema validation gate and strict type enforcement are critical before relaunch.
Reply to Mina's forwarded email using reply_to_email (email_id: "email_mina_forward_vectral_config_regression", sender: "lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com"). The reply should explain the root cause: Anh's config validation dry-run script on April 20 inadvertently called configStore.save() after loading each config into the preview renderer, writing normalized defaults back to the live store instead of a temp buffer. This reverted both Vectral and Mosaic configs to stale values, resetting the Vectral ergonomic limit from $600 to $400 and causing Danielle Osei's $490 monitor arm to be auto-rejected. Confirm that both clients are affected and that the stipend record has been corrected. Mention that the pipeline hardening work in ENG-217 is being updated to prevent this class of issue from recurring.
Post a summary message in #engineering (C003) using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C003). The message should give Samira the full picture before the April 30 readiness review: root cause of the config regression (Anh's dry-run script side-effect on April 20 writing to the live config store), confirmation that both Vectral and Mosaic configs were affected, the Danielle Osei stipend record has been corrected, Dmitri's blocking review on ENG-217 has been reviewed and a comment posted on the ticket addressing the race condition, and current status of the four blocking items for the readiness review. Also note that Greg Pallone at Swift reported vendor API 500 errors starting April 22 and a ticket has been created to track it.
Create a Linear issue for the vendor API 500 errors using linear_create_issue (team: "team_engineering", title referencing intermittent HTTP 500 errors on the vendor API since April 22, description covering the affected endpoints POST /api/v1/vendor/shipment-confirmation and POST /api/v1/vendor/delivery-receipt, approximately 60% failure rate on POST requests, 4 active shipments impacted, errors started around 3 PM Pacific on April 22 as reported by Greg Pallone at Swift Relocations, and that the timing should be investigated for any connection to the config store changes from Anh's script).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The Airtable stipend record rec_stipend_danielle_monitor_arm was updated (via airtable_update_records) in base appMoveOpsOps001, table tblStipends00001.
outcome

2 The Airtable update to rec_stipend_danielle_monitor_arm changes the Approval Status to 'Approved' (or similar corrected status) and sets the Approved Amount to 490.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The airtable_update_records call for rec_stipend_danielle_monitor_arm sets 'Approval Status': 'Approved' and 'Approved Amount': 490, and the tool result confirms these values were saved successfully.
✓Run #2The airtable_update_records call sets 'Approval Status': 'Approved' and 'Approved Amount': 490. The tool response confirms these values were saved.
✓Run #3The airtable_update_records call sets 'Approval Status' to 'Approved' and 'Approved Amount' to 490. The tool result confirms the fields were updated as expected.
✓Run #4The airtable_update_records fields parameter includes 'Approval Status': 'Approved' and 'Approved Amount': 490. The result confirms these values were written successfully.
✓Run #5The airtable_update_records call sets 'Approval Status' to 'Approved' and 'Approved Amount' to 490, confirmed by the tool result showing these values in the returned record.

3 The Airtable update review note (or Review Notes field) for rec_stipend_danielle_monitor_arm explains that the rejection was caused by a config regression or config reversion that applied the wrong ergonomic limit (e.g., $400 instead of the correct $600, config overwritten, config reverted, or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The Review Notes field in the update reads: 'Auto-rejected by ExpenseBot using stale $400 default limit. Vectral policy allows $600 for ergonomic equipment. Config regression confirmed — ingestion pipeline re-ran without hardening fix, reverting to default template values.' This clearly explains the config regression as the cause of the incorrect rejection.
✓Run #2The Review Notes field explicitly states: 'Config was reverted to old defaults by Anh's dashboard preview dry-run script (configStore.save() side effect overwrote corrected configs on ~Apr 20). $490 is within the correct $600 limit.' This clearly explains the config regression caused the incorrect $400 limit.

✗Run #6The email to Mina attributes the root cause to the ingestion pipeline (ENG-209) being re-run and hitting a merged-cell parse error that triggered silent fallback to defaults. 19 The final response identifies Dmitri Volkov's blocking review on the pipeline hardening PR (ENG-217) as concerning a race condition in validateSchema() or a concurrency/locking issue (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The final response states: 'Dmitri found a race condition in `validateSchema()`: during concurrent batch imports, the validator acquires a shared lock on the schema cache but doesn't hold it through the write phase.' This clearly identifies the race condition and concurrency/locking issue.
✓Run #2The final response states: 'Dmitri found a race condition in `validateSchema()` — if two policy configs load concurrently during batch import, the validator acquires a shared lock on the schema cache but doesn't hold it through the write phase.'
✓Run #3The final response explicitly describes 'Dmitri left a blocking review flagging a race condition in validateSchema(): when two policy configs load concurrently during batch import, the validator acquires a shared lock on the schema cache but doesn't hold it through the write phase.'
✓Run #4The final response explicitly states: 'Dmitri flagged a race condition in validateSchema(): during concurrent batch imports, the validator acquires a shared lock on the schema cache but doesn't hold it through the write phase, allowing a second config to slip through unvalidated.'
✓Run #5The final response states 'Dmitri left a blocking review on the pipeline hardening PR. He found a race condition in validateSchema(): when two policy configs are loaded concurrently during a batch import, the validator acquires a shared lock on the schema cache but doesn't hold it through the write phase.'
✓Run #6The final response explicitly identifies: 'Dmitri left a blocking review flagging a race condition in validateSchema(): when two policy configs are loaded concurrently during a batch import, the validator acquires a shared lock on the schema cache but doesn't hold it through the write phase.'

query construction
